American racing operation The Flying Dutchmen made their first Australian purchase at this week's Inglis Australian Easter Yearling Sale, but Hunter Rankin reveals they have had designs on the industry for much longer, drawn by the allure of one of the country's most elite sires.

A close relation to G1 Golden Slipper Stakes winner Mossfun, Harbour Fun was the most in-demand mare in this week's Magic Millions Digital Sale, where Proven Thoroughbreds' offering of shares in 18 horses was also popular.

A share in Extreme Choice headlines the 2026 Inglis Chairman’s Sale catalogue of 104 elite prospects. Group 1 winners Benedetta and Provence are joined by the dams of Guest House, Streisand, and Captured by Love.
A multiple Group 1-performed daughter of The Autumn Sun, Tutta La Vita will be offered in the Inglis Digital April Early Sale this month as a broodmare prospect by Resolute Racing.
Who impressed form analyst Nic Ashman in Saturday's Group 1 events, and who is getting crossed out of the black book? Aeliana on the back up is his key highlight from a stellar weekend of racing.

Breeders left with an ineligible foal have been given a respite with Racing Australia announcing an amnesty for 2024 and 2025 born foals. All previously ineligible foals are now eligible to race, even if the appeal was declined, and those who haven’t done their FODs have until April 14, 2026 to ensure their foal can race.

The catalogue for the 2026 Inglis Australian Weanling Sale has been released. The final crop of Snitzel is represented, as well as opportunities to purchase the offspring of first-season sires such as Shinzo and Ozzmosis in a big book of 500 lots. The sale will take place on May 5 and 6 at the Riverside Stables complex, ahead of the Inglis Chairman’s Sale on Thursday, May 7.

As a key sale on the Australian calendar, the Inglis Easter Yearling Sale results demonstrate the health of the top end of the market. Held in 2026 under a difficult economic climate, the lower clearance rate led to a lower gross, but the popular lots sold well pushing up the average.
